item.title: Raspberry Pi profit surges as AI boom lifts demand
item.description: Sales growth for UK computer company particularly strong in China and US

item.title: UK growers warn of cucumber and tomato shortages as gas prices surge
item.description: Producers warn of empty shelves if supermarkets do not raise prices to help offset costs linked to Iran war

item.title: Unilever nears deal to merge food unit with McCormick to create $60bn group
item.description: Move will transform Hellmann’s mayonnaise maker from multi-category conglomerate into beauty and personal care company

item.title: Inflation worries become growth worries
item.description: A longer war makes long bonds interesting

item.title: Aviation regulator rejects Heathrow’s £10bn spending plan
item.description: Civil Aviation Authority says average charges per passenger should stay broadly flat over next five years

item.title: Asia turns to coal as Iran war chokes off gas supplies
item.description: Countries shrug off environmental concerns to fire up use of polluting fuel as Gulf supplies dwindle

item.title: FT Alphaville’s toy Collateralised Whatever Obligations
item.description: The FTAV Credit Explainer Part II: Securitisation Bugaloo

item.title: Private capital’s great disappointment era begins
item.description: The $22tn industry is facing the prospect that returns will fall far short of the expectations of investors

item.title: Foreign central banks sell US Treasuries in wake of Iran war
item.description: International official holdings at New York Federal Reserve fall to lowest level since 2012

item.title: BlackRock looks at HSBC’s Canary Wharf tower for new London HQ
item.description: Asset manager is seeking at least 600,000 sq ft as demand for high-end offices rises

item.title: In the age of AI, the US must rethink the taxation of labour and capital
item.description: As the population ages and technology disrupts the economy, young workers are carrying a disproportionate tax burden Tax

item.title: BlackRock to boost investment in NHS property
item.description: Asset manager teams up with Greater Manchester Pension Fund for £1bn joint venture as government seeks more private finance

item.title: Swiss lawmakers signal compromise on $22bn UBS capital plan
item.description: Decision could be published in April with foreign capital requirements component moving to parliament for debate

item.title: ‘Pump anxiety’ from soaring fuel prices prompts surge in EV interest
item.description: Increased costs since outbreak of Middle East conflict prompt more test drives and advert views

item.title: UK chip start-up Fractile seeks to raise $200mn to challenge Nvidia
item.description: Company part of growing cohort of UK groups developing faster AI processors

item.title: When did democracy become an investment bubble?
item.description: A new closed-end fund is open to anyone with a brokerage account but whether it brings prosperity remains to be seen

item.title: The City law firm that found wealth in private capital
item.description: Macfarlanes has transcended its roots to become one of London’s most profitable legal partnerships

item.title: ‘C-Reit’ gives global investors a route out of Chinese property
item.description: CapitaLand of Singapore was the first international sponsor of a Chinese real estate investment trust

item.title: Fallen hedge fund Pelham Capital plots back-to-basics comeback
item.description: Founder Ross Turner to put $100mn into new fund to be managed by Kayvan Larizadeh

item.title: India’s film industry searches for a script beyond stardom
item.description: Bollywood has just made one of its biggest blockbusters. But insiders say the old system is struggling

item.title: Top energy developer warns on overbuilding power supplies for AI
item.description: David Crane says data centres should bear the cost of developing infrastructure to serve their demand

item.title: The rocky legal future of Trump’s tariffs
item.description: In the second installment of our tariff series, we talk about how the courts are making a mess of the White House’s global trade policy

item.title: Pete Hegseth’s broker looked to buy defence fund before Iran attack
item.description: Morgan Stanley wealth manager approached BlackRock about multimillion-dollar investment for US defence secretary

item.title: US petrol prices hit $4 a gallon as war jolts energy markets
item.description: A Kuwaiti oil tanker was hit off Dubai as Iran launched more strikes across the Gulf

item.title: Is Avi Lewis’s NDP doomed? Don’t write the obituary just yet
item.description: More than a few voters could find themselves unsatisfied with the Liberals and open to a hard-left turn in future elections

item.title: India struggles to stem the rupee’s fall
item.description: Also in this newsletter, mining conglomerate Vedanta heads for demerger

item.title: Sun Life to pay almost $3-billion for stakes in private credit and real estate management firms
item.description: Chief executive Kevin Strain says BGO and Crescent are ‘integral’ to overall growth strategy

item.title: Generic drugmaker Apotex switches CEO ahead of planned IPO
item.description: Jeff Watson, who led Apotex from 2018 to 2023, will replace Allan Oberman, who is departing from the role

item.title: Air Canada employees and investors deserve better than Michael Rousseau
item.description: The new CEO needs French and a lot more to improve results for the airline, which trails most of its peers

item.title: Some Metro employees begin strike in Quebec citing pay, working conditions as main concern
item.description: The grocer said it has implemented a contingency plan to keep business running as normal

item.title: Flight attendant injured in LaGuardia crash continues recovery as GoFundMe page raises over $188,000
item.description: The fundraiser for Solange Tremblay was launched by her daughter, Sarah Lépine

item.title: Distressed-debt funds target private credit downturn as ‘greatest opportunity’ since 2008
item.description: Investors anticipate moneymaking bonanza as sector comes under strain

item.title: McCain’s ownership flaws were identified decades ago – now a second generation is feuding all over again
item.description: Eleanor McCain is suing relatives for allegedly thwarting her efforts to sell her $1-billion stake in the company

item.title: Air Canada learned nothing during the five years since its last French scandal
item.description: Though the LaGuardia accident doesn’t appear to be the fault of Air Canada, it has revealed other problems at Canada’s national airline

item.title: UK regulator cuts £2bn from car finance redress costs for banks
item.description: Financial Conduct Authority lowers estimate for loans eligible for compensation scheme

item.title: GM to add sixth day at Michigan plant to meet strong demand for heavy-duty trucks
item.description: The auto maker sold about 320,000 Silverado and Sierra pickups in the U.S. last year

item.title: Young workers will be hurt most by AI, but they’ll also reap the benefits
item.description: Playing field between employees is levelled, as AI gives new hires access to knowledge that would have taken years to obtain

item.title: Air Canada employee charged in alleged cannabis smuggling plot at Toronto Pearson
item.description: Police allege an employee working in the baggage room placed tags on luggage belonging to two unsuspecting victims that contained the cannabis

item.title: Crypto has no place in UK political donations
item.description: Its inclusion will not extend democracy but reduce it

item.title: Qatar-backed US LNG plant starts production as Iran war hits global supply
item.description: Golden Pass plant owned by QatarEnergy and ExxonMobil may help replace shortages hit by Hormuz crisis

item.title: After Iran, gold is looking less glittery
item.description: Is the yellow metal the new crypto?

item.title: Federal court denies Competition Bureau access to Amazon data
item.description: Commissioner wanted data on transaction information for health, personal care, home and electronic items sold on Amazon’s platforms

item.title: Trump to take first steps in opening retirement funds to private markets
item.description: US Department of Labor to offer plan administrators safe harbour process for selecting alternative investments

item.title: Federal, Ontario governments to spend $8.8-billion to cut municipal development charges
item.description: Plan to lower charges by up to 50% would remain in place for three years with the goal of spurring homebuilding

item.title: A blueprint for Chinese global leadership
item.description: With the US destroying its own credibility, the opportunity is Beijing’s for the taking Adam Tooze

item.title: Alberta business leaders call for regulatory overhaul for industrial projects
item.description: Business Council of Alberta made a series of recommendations it says will allow big-ticket developments to be built faster

item.title: BP loses head of EV charging as it accelerates pivot back to oil and gas
item.description: Martin Thomsen’s departure comes ahead of arrival of oil major’s new chief executive this week

item.title: Subscriber webinar on May 20: the dollar under Trump
item.description: Join FT journalists to discuss the greenback, inflation, and markets under a new Fed chair

item.title: Air Canada CEO Michael Rousseau to step down
item.description: Rousseau, whose English-only condolence video sparked controversy after the LaGuardia Airport crash, will retire by the end of September

item.title: Indigenous-owned Pure Casino bids for Gamehost with backing from U.S. real estate giant
item.description: Company would partly finance the deal by selling Gamehost’s four Alberta properties to U.S.-based VICI Properties

item.title: Sysco to buy catering supplier Jetro Restaurant Depot in $29-billion deal
item.description: Deal will be funded through $21-billion of new and hybrid debt, along with $1-billion of cash and equity

item.title: Canada’s biggest banks get into defence after years of avoiding military financing
item.description: The post Canada’s biggest banks get into defence after years of avoiding military financing appeared first on The Logic .

item.title: Powell says Fed positioned to ‘wait and see’ how Iran war affects economy, inflation
item.description: If the Fed raises rates to get ahead of inflation, it risks slowing the economy and hurting employment

item.title: Why food prices are resisting the oil and fertiliser shock — for now
item.description: Good harvests and plentiful grain stocks will provide some cushion against the impact of the Iran war Alan Beattie

item.title: WTO suffers fresh blow as reform push hits a wall at Cameroon meeting
item.description: Four days of talks ended without agreement on a plan for reform or even on extending a moratorium on e-commerce duties

item.title: The Bank of Canada is ramping up its audits of payments companies
item.description: The post The Bank of Canada is ramping up its audits of payments companies appeared first on The Logic .

item.title: Who Struck It Rich in the Markets When Trump Postponed Bombing Iran?
item.description: A series of uncannily timed bets on the price of oil and stocks deserves a proper investigation. It’s far from clear that they’ll get one.

item.title: Trump threatens destruction of Iran’s energy resources if a deal isn’t reached soon
item.description: Iran strikes key water and electrical plant in Kuwait, Israeli oil refinery as war rages with no end in sight

item.title: In the Iran oil shock, energy superpower Canada must seize the day
item.description: Canada must take the lead in forging a new global energy security pact

item.title: Is THG about to make some mAjOr gAiNs? Over nearly 2,000 words, we struggle to say
item.description: Bulk bye Tax

item.title: What to do when the ‘public good’ of information goes bad
item.description: The creation and dissemination of reliable news is at an economic disadvantage Martin Wolf

item.title: Start-up founders shrink from green pitches at progressive SXSW
item.description: Focus shifts from climate-led framing to resilience and risk management

item.title: How Trump's tariffs ripped up the global trade order
item.description: In the first installment of a new FTNB series this week, we explore how President Trump’s so-called Liberation Day has shaken up the global trade order Alan Beattie

item.title: Battle over Ottawa’s new vehicle regulations heats up
item.description: New rules could help shape country’s automotive market for the next decade

item.title: The plan to make IPOs great again
item.description: America’s regulators and market operators are teaming up to rekindle public listings

item.title: How Iran is making a mint from Donald Trump’s war
item.description: China is helping the Revolutionary Guards profit from Iranian crude

item.title: Korea weighs inheritance tax based on book value, not market prices
item.description: Government proposal aims to curb the alleged suppression of share prices and reduce the ‘Korea discount’ Tax
